problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Optogenetic. Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 28 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: To know that you do not know is the best. — Lao Tzu
